name: SpectrumAnalyzer
author: Nguyen
description: Runs spectrum analysis on the whole library, album by album: detects fake lossless / lossy transcodes via frequency-cutoff heuristics and stores a spectrogram (base64 PNG) per song. Tracks are analyzed once and re-analyzed only when the underlying file changes; results are removed automatically by the core cleanup task.

versions.changelog: New UPSAMPLED verdict for fake hi-res: a >48 kHz container whose content stops at CD bandwidth (with digital silence above) is flagged as a resampled 44.1/48 kHz source instead of CLEAN. UPSAMPLED counts as suspect in the overview filters.

Parallel scans: one task per album fanned out across all workers, with a parent task tracking done/remaining. Fewer false FAKE_SUSPECT verdicts: content reaching ~20.5 kHz counts as full bandwidth, and a sharp edge with audible noise above the cutoff is now LOWPASSED, not FAKE. UI: filter albums by suspect count, re-analyze a whole album from its page.

Initial release: library scan grouped by album, change detection (metadata fingerprint + audio MD5), fake-lossless verdicts, base64 spectrograms, per-track re-analysis, on_song_analyzed hook, optional weekly incremental cron.
